payload.answer: Knowledge graphs are structured data models using nodes (entities) and edges (relationships) to represent interconnected information. They power search engines, recommendation systems, and semantic applications. What specific aspect would you like to explore—technical architecture, use cases, or tools?
payload.decision: Create foundational memory cards about knowledge graphs with high-level concepts and defer specialized details pending user clarification

entity_resolution.reason: User intent is exploratory; specific knowledge graph systems or domains not yet identified

memory_cards.statement: A knowledge graph is a structured representation of information that uses nodes (entities) and edges (relationships) to model real-world concepts and their interconnections
Knowledge graphs are used in search engines, recommendation systems, semantic web applications, and enterprise data management

repair_options: Clarify whether user seeks technical implementation details, business applications, or specific knowledge graph platforms
Request domain context (e.g., enterprise, web, AI/ML) to tailor subsequent learning
